docs.title: Meridian Docs
docs.text:
Meridian is a local-first CLI and GitHub Action for reviewing OpenTelemetry Collector configuration changes before merge.

It combines:

  • repo-side validation
  • Collector-native semantic validation
  • graph generation
  • diff-aware review hints
  • deterministic runtime checks
  • assertion and contract evaluation
  • durable artifacts for local debugging and pull-request review

docs.title: Start here
docs.text:
• New user: Quickstart
  • Need install details: Install
  • Want the exact command surface: CLI Reference
  • Integrating CI: GitHub Action task guide
  • Maintaining the project: Contributor Guide

docs.title: Product model
docs.text:
Meridian is built around three evidence layers:

  1. Repo-side parsing and structure checks on locally materializable config sources.
  2. Collector-native semantic validation against the selected binary or image.
  3. Deterministic runtime execution against a patched config with capture-backed assertions.

The default path is safe mode. It proves flow and reviewability without sending synthetic telemetry to real backends.

docs.title: Documentation structure
docs.text:
This site follows an OSS documentation split similar to large infrastructure projects:

  • Overview: what Meridian is and how to get started
  • Concepts: product behavior and mental models
  • Tasks: focused how-to guides
  • Tutorials: end-to-end guided flows
  • Reference: exact contracts, flags, and artifacts
  • Contribute: maintainer and documentation workflows

docs.title: Artifact Model
docs.text: Artifacts are first-class Meridian output. They are the durable evidence for what happened during a run.

docs.title: Layout
docs.text:
Runtime runs use:

meridian-artifacts/
  runs/
    latest -> <run_id>
    <run_id>/

latest resolves relative to the selected --output directory and is used by the debug commands when --run is omitted.

docs.title: Core runtime artifacts
docs.text:
Every runtime-oriented run aims to preserve:

  • report.json
  • summary.md
  • config.patched.yaml
  • graph.mmd
  • collector.log
  • captures/

Additional artifacts appear when the run produces that evidence:

  • config.final.yaml
  • collector-components.json
  • semantic-findings.json
  • graph.svg
  • diff.md
  • contracts.json
  • contracts.md
  • capture.normalized.json

docs.title: Provenance model
docs.text:
Meridian reports three related config views:

  • original config sources
  • runtime config provenance, which explains whether runtime patching used source-merged or Collector-rendered config
  • final patched config used for execution

That distinction is important when config URIs or print-config are involved.

docs.title: Diff-Aware Review
docs.text: Meridian can compare two Collector configs and highlight risky changes for reviewers.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text:
Diff-aware review supports:

  • explicit file paths via --old and --new
  • git refs via --base and --head
  • severity filtering with --severity-threshold

Runtime workflows can also enable --changed-only to restrict review hints to explicit diff inputs.

docs.title: Classification focus
docs.text:
Meridian classifies changes in:

  • pipeline wiring
  • processors
  • connectors
  • extensions
  • auth and TLS-related config
  • service.telemetry

docs.title: Effective config preference
docs.text: When Collector print-config is available, Meridian prefers effective-config diffs over raw source diffs. This improves review quality for configs that rely on includes, URI sources, or Collector rendering behavior.

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text:
Diff data appears in:

  • report.json
  • summary.md
  • diff.md when changes exist

The goal is reviewer guidance, not a generic YAML diff replacement.

docs.title: K3s Fixture
docs.text: The repository-owned fixture under examples/k3s-e2e/ is Meridian's authoritative real-cluster regression target.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
The fixture exists to validate Meridian itself against a small, editable, deterministic Kubernetes stack.

It is not intended to become a generic Kubernetes testing framework.

docs.title: Fixture roles
docs.text:
• storefront: entrypoint service
  • checkout: downstream request handler
  • inventory: reserve handler plus heartbeat metrics
  • trafficgen: repeatable workload driver

docs.title: Scenarios
docs.text:
• happy
  • drop-traces
  • misroute-logs
  • auth-fail
  • backend-unreachable

Negative scenarios are successful when the expected failure mode is detected correctly.

docs.title: Primary acceptance signals
docs.text:
On the project VM, the blocking signals are:

  • pod logs
  • Prometheus evidence
  • gateway counters

Tempo and Loki artifacts remain supporting evidence when available, but they are not always run-scoped enough to be the only acceptance gate.

docs.title: Runtime Engines
docs.text: Meridian supports auto, docker, and containerd.

docs.title: Auto resolution
docs.text: auto prefers Docker when both engines are available. This keeps behavior stable for common local and CI environments.

docs.title: Docker
docs.text:
Docker is the default expectation for:

  • local test and check
  • GitHub Actions examples
  • semantic validation via image when no local Collector binary is provided

For Docker, Meridian routes capture traffic through a host alias.

docs.title: Linux containerd
docs.text: On Linux, --engine containerd expects nerdctl and uses host networking for the capture path.

docs.title: macOS containerd
docs.text:
On macOS, --engine containerd uses lima nerdctl, published injection ports, and host.lima.internal for capture sink routing.

Direct nerdctl reuse of an OrbStack Docker context is not part of Meridian's supported contract.

docs.title: Runtime Modes
docs.text: Meridian supports three runtime modes: safe, tee, and live.

docs.title: Safe
docs.text:
safe is the default and recommended mode.

Behavior:

  • injects a Meridian-managed OTLP receiver
  • injects a Meridian-managed OTLP capture exporter
  • replaces pipeline receivers with the injected receiver
  • replaces pipeline exporters with the capture exporter
  • preserves connector exporters

What it proves:

  • the config wires correctly
  • processors still let telemetry pass
  • selected signals arrive at the capture sink
  • assertions and contracts match normalized captured output

What it does not prove:

  • real exporter connectivity
  • vendor auth correctness
  • production routing to live backends

docs.title: Tee
docs.text:
tee preserves original exporters and appends the Meridian capture exporter.

Use it when you want more realistic exporter behavior while still keeping Meridian evidence.

docs.title: Live
docs.text:
live also preserves real exporters and appends Meridian capture.

Warning

live may send synthetic telemetry to real destinations. Use it deliberately and only when that is acceptable for the target environment.

docs.title: Runtime patching
docs.text:
Runtime patching depends on a locally materialized config:

  • source-merged config when every source is locally materializable
  • Collector-rendered print-config output when the config includes non-materializable URIs

If neither path is available, runtime preparation fails clearly before container execution starts.

docs.title: Semantic Validation
docs.text: Semantic validation is Meridian's Collector-native check layer. It validates the config against an actual Collector distribution before runtime execution.

docs.title: Target resolution
docs.text:
Meridian resolves the semantic target in this order:

  1. --collector-binary
  2. --collector-image using the selected engine
  3. skip semantic validation when the command allows it and no target is available

docs.title: Stages
docs.text:
Meridian surfaces explicit semantic stages:

  • components
  • validate
  • print-config

print-config is best effort and uses the otelcol.printInitialConfig feature gate. Meridian reports when that stage is skipped, unsupported, or fails.

docs.title: Why this matters
docs.text:
Repo-side parsing alone cannot prove that the selected Collector build:

  • supports the referenced components
  • accepts the actual config schema
  • emits usable effective config for diffing and runtime provenance

docs.title: Evidence artifacts
docs.text:
When available, semantic validation writes:

  • collector-components.json
  • semantic-findings.json
  • config.final.yaml

Those files complement report.json and summary.md.

docs.title: URI-only configs
docs.text: If every config source is a Collector-native URI such as yaml: or file:, repo-side parsing may be skipped. In that case, semantic validation becomes the primary static evidence path.

docs.title: Development loop
docs.text:
go test ./...
go build ./...

When changing user-facing CLI help or commands, regenerate the docs:

go run ./cmd/meridian-docs

docs.title: Code ownership guidelines
docs.text:
• keep validation rules in internal/validate
  • keep capture behavior in internal/capture
  • keep telemetry generation in internal/generator
  • keep runtime orchestration in internal/runtime
  • keep reporting renderers pure where possible

docs.title: Reproducing CI failures
docs.text:
• download the artifact bundle
  • inspect report.json and collector.log
  • rerun locally with --keep-containers

docs.title: Docs work
docs.text: See Docs Authoring for the local MkDocs workflow and Release Docs Workflow for publishing behavior.

docs.title: Docs Authoring
docs.text: Meridian documentation is built with MkDocs, Material for MkDocs, and mike.

docs.title: Local workflow
docs.text:
python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
python -m pip install -r docs/requirements.txt
go run ./cmd/meridian-docs
mkdocs serve

docs.title: Rules
docs.text:
• write hand-authored content under the docs IA sections
  • do not hand-edit files under docs/reference/cli/ except index.md
  • update Go command help text, then regenerate CLI docs
  • keep examples short and copy-pasteable
  • prefer task-focused docs over long narrative dumps

docs.title: CI expectations
docs.text:
The docs workflow:

  • regenerates CLI docs
  • fails if generated files drift
  • runs mkdocs build --strict

docs.title: Release Docs Workflow
docs.text: Documentation publishing is separate from the binary release workflow.

docs.title: Branch and tag behavior
docs.text:
• pull requests build the site and fail on docs drift
  • pushes to main publish dev
  • v* tags publish <major>.<minor> and update latest

docs.title: Maintainer responsibilities
docs.text:
• keep generated CLI reference checked in
  • make docs changes in the same PR as behavior changes
  • ensure release notes and docs agree on user-facing contract changes

docs.title: Why versioned docs
docs.text: Meridian's CLI and artifact contract are part of its user interface. Versioned docs reduce drift between release binaries and the published reference.

docs.title: Build from source
docs.text:
Meridian is a single Go binary.

go build -o ./bin/meridian ./cmd/meridian

Requirements:

  • Go 1.25+ to build from source
  • Docker for the default runtime path
  • nerdctl for Linux --engine containerd
  • Lima for macOS --engine containerd via lima nerdctl
  • Graphviz dot only when rendering SVG graphs

docs.title: Install a release binary
docs.text:
curl -fsSL -o meridian.tar.gz https://github.com/salman-frs/meridian/releases/latest/download/meridian-linux-amd64.tar.gz
tar -xzf meridian.tar.gz
./meridian version

Release artifacts are published by the repository release workflow and include checksums.

docs.title: Install docs tooling
docs.text:
For local docs work:

python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
python -m pip install -r docs/requirements.txt
go run ./cmd/meridian-docs
mkdocs serve

docs.title: Install matrix
docs.text: Use case Minimum requirement validate with local YAML only Go build plus local files validate with semantic image checks Docker or containerd plus Collector image pull access graph --render=svg Graphviz dot test, check, ci Supported container engine --engine containerd on macOS Lima with lima nerdctl

docs.title: Output layout
docs.text: Runtime and debug-oriented commands use ./meridian-artifacts by default and accept --output to choose a different artifact root.

docs.title: Quickstart
docs.text: This is the shortest path to a meaningful Meridian run.

docs.title: Build the CLI
docs.text: go build -o ./bin/meridian ./cmd/meridian

docs.title: Run static validation
docs.text:
./bin/meridian validate -c examples/basic/collector.yaml

What this does:

  • loads local config sources when available
  • interpolates env values from --env-file, --env, and the shell
  • runs repo-side validation
  • runs Collector-native semantic validation when a binary or image is available

docs.title: Run the opinionated confidence workflow
docs.text:
./bin/meridian check -c examples/basic/collector.yaml

This performs:

  • semantic validation
  • graph artifact generation
  • runtime patching in safe mode
  • synthetic telemetry injection
  • capture-backed assertions
  • markdown and JSON artifact generation

docs.title: Inspect the latest run
docs.text:
By default, runtime artifacts land under ./meridian-artifacts/runs/<run_id>/, with ./meridian-artifacts/runs/latest/ pointing at the newest run.

Useful follow-ups:

./bin/meridian debug summary
./bin/meridian debug bundle --format json
./bin/meridian debug logs
./bin/meridian debug capture

docs.title: Next steps
docs.text:
• Learn the install and runtime prerequisites in Install
  • Understand safe vs tee vs live in Runtime Modes
  • See the full artifact contract in Artifact Contract

docs.title: Release Policy
docs.text:
Meridian publishes two documentation tracks:

  • dev: built from the current main branch
  • release docs: published from v* tags as <major>.<minor> with a latest alias

The docs site uses mike for versioned publishing.

docs.title: Supported docs versions
docs.text:
The target policy is:

  • keep dev
  • keep latest
  • keep the current release plus the previous four release doc versions

If a documentation change only affects unreleased behavior, it belongs in dev first.

docs.title: Release binary policy
docs.text:
The binary release workflow currently publishes:

  • darwin and linux
  • amd64 and arm64
  • tarballs plus checksums.txt

docs.title: Backward-compatibility expectations
docs.text: Docs should preserve stable top-level CLI verbs and describe behavior that matches the checked-in code for that version. When behavior differs by version, release docs should document the released contract, not main.

docs.title: Reference
docs.text: Use this section when you need exact contracts rather than tutorials.

docs.title: Included reference sets
docs.text:
• CLI Reference
  • GitHub Action
  • Artifact Contract
  • Config Inputs
  • Examples
  • Exit Codes

docs.title: GitHub Action Reference
docs.text: The composite action lives at action/action.yml.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Input Required Default Notes config yes none Collector config path in the checked-out workspace engine no auto auto, docker, containerd mode no safe safe, tee, live collector_image no pinned contrib image Used for runtime execution timeout no 30s Overall runtime timeout env_file no empty Optional dotenv file assertions no empty v1 assertions or v2 contracts file render_graph no mermaid Runtime graph artifact mode comment_mode no update off, create, update artifact_retention_days no 7 Passed to artifact upload

docs.title: Outputs and side effects
docs.text:
The action does not publish custom action outputs. Its side effects are:

  • uploaded artifacts
  • a GitHub step summary
  • an optional PR comment update

docs.title: Permissions
docs.text: For PR comment updates, the calling workflow needs pull-requests: write.

docs.title: Artifact Contract
docs.text: This page documents the runtime bundle and k3s fixture contracts that Meridian currently preserves.

docs.title: Runtime bundle
docs.text:
Standard files:

  • report.json
  • summary.md
  • config.patched.yaml
  • graph.mmd
  • collector.log
  • captures/

Conditional files:

  • config.final.yaml when Collector print-config succeeded
  • collector-components.json when component inventory was collected
  • semantic-findings.json when semantic findings exist
  • graph.svg when SVG rendering is requested and Graphviz is available
  • diff.md when diff data exists
  • contracts.json
  • contracts.md
  • capture.normalized.json

docs.title: Runtime provenance fields
docs.text:
report.json records:

  • original config source
  • runtime config source
  • engine and runtime backend
  • timings
  • findings, diff, graph, semantic, assertions, contracts, and captures
  • bundle paths

docs.title: latest symlink
docs.text: Meridian maintains runs/latest as a symlink to the most recent runtime run under the selected output root.

docs.title: K3s fixture artifacts
docs.text:
Per scenario, the VM runner preserves:

  • summary.md
  • summary.json
  • logs/*.log
  • kubectl-get-all.txt
  • events.txt
  • prom-http-requests.json
  • prom-checkout.json
  • prom-heartbeat.json
  • loki-storefront.json
  • tempo-storefront.json

docs.title: Config Inputs
docs.text: Meridian supports multiple ways to provide Collector config.

docs.title: Repeatable --config
docs.text:
--config is repeatable and accepts:

  • local file paths
  • Collector-native config URIs

Example:

./bin/meridian validate \
  -c collector.yaml \
  -c yaml:exporters::otlp::endpoint=localhost:4317

docs.title: --config-dir
docs.text:
Use --config-dir when your Collector config is already rendered into a directory layout.

./bin/meridian validate --config-dir ./rendered-config

docs.title: Env interpolation
docs.text:
Meridian resolves env values from:

  1. --env-file
  2. repeatable --env KEY=value
  3. exported shell env vars

Repo-side parsing tracks referenced env names and reports missing values clearly.

docs.title: Collector-native validation target
docs.text:
For semantic validation:

  • use --collector-binary to point at a specific local Collector build
  • otherwise Meridian can use --collector-image through the selected engine

docs.title: URI-only runtime constraints
docs.text:
Runtime commands can work with URI-only inputs only when Meridian can materialize a local runtime config:

  • directly from locally materializable sources
  • or from Collector print-config

If neither path exists, runtime preparation fails before execution.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: The repository ships example configs and fixtures under examples/.

docs.title: CLI examples
docs.text:
• examples/basic/collector.yaml
  • examples/routing/collector.yaml
  • examples/redaction/collector.yaml
  • examples/multipipeline/collector.yaml

docs.title: Assertion example
docs.text: • examples/basic/assertions.yaml

docs.title: Contract fixture example
docs.text: • examples/redaction/assertions.yaml

docs.title: K3s fixture
docs.text: • examples/k3s-e2e/

docs.title: Exit Codes
docs.text: Meridian keeps exit semantics scriptable and simple.

docs.title: Stable codes
docs.text: Code Meaning 0 Success 1 Unclassified failure 2 User input, validation, or contract/assertion loading failure 3 Runtime, semantic execution, artifact, or environment failure

docs.title: Guidance
docs.text:
Treat exit codes as coarse failure classes. For exact cause, inspect:

  • stderr
  • summary.md
  • report.json
  • bundle artifacts such as collector.log

docs.title: CLI Reference
docs.text: These pages are generated from the Cobra command tree with go run ./cmd/meridian-docs. Do not hand-edit the generated command pages. Update command help text in Go code, then regenerate.

docs.title: Commands
docs.text:
• meridian: Review and runtime-test OpenTelemetry Collector configs
  • meridian check: Run Meridian's opinionated end-to-end confidence workflow
  • meridian ci: CI-friendly compatibility wrapper around check
  • meridian completion: Generate shell completion scripts
  • meridian debug: Inspect artifacts from a previous Meridian run
  • meridian debug bundle: Print the run bundle manifest
  • meridian debug capture: Print capture samples from a run directory
  • meridian debug logs: Print collector logs from a run directory
  • meridian debug summary: Print the stored markdown summary
  • meridian diff: Compare two collector configs and classify risky changes
  • meridian graph: Build a pipeline graph for the collector config
  • meridian test: Run the runtime harness against the collector config
  • meridian validate: Run static validation against a collector config
  • meridian version: Print the Meridian version

docs.title: meridian
docs.text: Review and runtime-test OpenTelemetry Collector configs

docs.title: Options
docs.text:
--collector-binary string   path to a collector binary used for semantic validation
  -c, --config stringArray        collector config source; repeatable and may be a file path or collector config URI
      --config-dir string         path to a rendered collector config directory
      --env stringArray           inline KEY=VALUE env vars
      --env-file string           dotenv file used for config interpolation
      --format string             output format: human|json (default "human")
  -h, --help                      help for meridian
      --quiet                     suppress human progress output
      --verbose                   enable verbose output

docs.title: SEE ALSO
docs.text:
• meridian check - Run Meridian's opinionated end-to-end confidence workflow
  • meridian ci - CI-friendly compatibility wrapper around check
  • meridian completion - Generate shell completion scripts
  • meridian debug - Inspect artifacts from a previous Meridian run
  • meridian diff - Compare two collector configs and classify risky changes
  • meridian graph - Build a pipeline graph for the collector config
  • meridian test - Run the runtime harness against the collector config
  • meridian validate - Run static validation against a collector config
  • meridian version - Print the Meridian version

docs.title: meridian check
docs.text:
Run Meridian's opinionated end-to-end confidence workflow

meridian check [flags]

docs.title: Options
docs.text:
--assertions string           assertions or contracts YAML file
      --base string                 git base ref used to materialize the old config
      --capture-samples int         maximum captured telemetry samples to persist per signal (default 5)
      --capture-timeout duration    capture wait timeout (default 10s)
      --changed-only                require explicit diff inputs and include only diff-aware review hints
      --collector-image string      collector image to run (default "otel/opentelemetry-collector-contrib@sha256:e7c92c715f28ff142f3bcaccd4fc5603cf4c71276ef09954a38eb4038500a5a5")
      --engine string               container engine: auto|docker|containerd (default "auto")
      --head string                 git head ref used to materialize the new config
  -h, --help                        help for check
      --inject-timeout duration     telemetry injection timeout (default 5s)
      --keep-containers             keep the collector container running after the test
      --mode string                 runtime mode: safe|tee|live (default "safe")
      --new string                  new collector config file used for diff-aware review
      --old string                  old collector config file used for diff-aware review
      --output string               artifact output directory (default "./meridian-artifacts")
      --pipelines strings           limit runtime checks to specific signals or pipelines
      --render-graph string         additional graph artifact for runtime commands: mermaid|svg|none (default "mermaid")
      --seed int                    deterministic generation seed (default 42)
      --severity-threshold string   minimum diff severity threshold: low|medium|high (default "low")
      --startup-timeout duration    collector startup timeout (default 10s)
      --timeout duration            overall runtime timeout (default 30s)

      --format string             output format: human|json (default "human")
      --quiet                     suppress human progress output
      --verbose                   enable verbose output

      --engine string               container engine used for collector image semantic validation: auto|docker|containerd (default "auto")
      --head string                 git head ref used to materialize the new config
  -h, --help                        help for diff

      --old string                  old collector config file used for diff-aware review
      --severity-threshold string   minimum diff severity threshold: low|medium|high (default "low")

docs.title: meridian test
docs.text:
Run the runtime harness against the collector config

meridian test [flags]

docs.title: Options
docs.text:
--assertions string          assertions or contracts YAML file
      --capture-samples int        maximum captured telemetry samples to persist per signal (default 5)
      --capture-timeout duration   capture wait timeout (default 10s)
      --changed-only               require explicit diff inputs and include only diff-aware review hints
      --collector-image string     collector image to run (default "otel/opentelemetry-collector-contrib@sha256:e7c92c715f28ff142f3bcaccd4fc5603cf4c71276ef09954a38eb4038500a5a5")
      --engine string              container engine: auto|docker|containerd (default "auto")
  -h, --help                       help for test
      --inject-timeout duration    telemetry injection timeout (default 5s)
      --keep-containers            keep the collector container running after the test
      --mode string                runtime mode: safe|tee|live (default "safe")
      --output string              artifact output directory (default "./meridian-artifacts")
      --pipelines strings          limit runtime checks to specific signals or pipelines
      --render-graph string        additional graph artifact for runtime commands: mermaid|svg|none (default "mermaid")
      --seed int                   deterministic generation seed (default 42)
      --startup-timeout duration   collector startup timeout (default 10s)
      --timeout duration           overall runtime timeout (default 30s)

docs.text: • meridian - Review and runtime-test OpenTelemetry Collector configs

docs.title: Write Assertions And Contracts
docs.text: Meridian supports legacy v1 assertions and richer v2 contracts and fixtures through the same --assertions flag.

docs.title: Use v1 assertions
docs.text:
v1 is a lightweight flow-check format.

version: 1
defaults:
  timeout: 10s
  min_count: 1

assertions:
  - id: traces-flow
    severity: fail
    signal: traces
    where:
      attributes:
        meridian.run_id: "{{RUN_ID}}"
    expect:
      min_count: 1

Supported filters:

  • attributes
  • span_name
  • metric_name
  • body

Supported expectations:

  • min_count
  • exists
  • attributes_present
  • attributes_absent

docs.title: Use v2 contracts
docs.text:
v2 is for reviewer-facing contract testing against normalized captured telemetry.

version: 2
defaults:
  min_count: 1

fixtures:
  - redaction

contracts:
  - id: no-authorization-header
    severity: fail
    signal: traces
    fixture: redaction
    expect:
      attributes_absent:
        - http.request.header.authorization

Generated contract artifacts:

  • contracts.json
  • contracts.md
  • capture.normalized.json

docs.title: Run with an assertions file
docs.text: ./bin/meridian check -c collector.yaml --assertions assertions.yaml

docs.title: Use Docker
docs.text:
Use Docker when:

  • you want the default, best-supported path
  • you are on GitHub-hosted Linux runners
  • you do not need explicit containerd coverage
./bin/meridian check -c collector.yaml --engine docker

docs.title: Use Linux containerd
docs.text:
Use Linux containerd when you specifically need nerdctl parity or a containerd-backed environment.

./bin/meridian check -c collector.yaml --engine containerd

Verify first:

nerdctl version

docs.title: Use macOS containerd
docs.text:
On macOS, Meridian expects Lima:

lima nerdctl version
./bin/meridian check -c collector.yaml --engine containerd

If you have both Docker and Lima installed, remember that --engine auto still prefers Docker.

docs.title: Runtime startup failures
docs.text:
If the Collector does not become ready:

  • inspect collector.log
  • inspect config.patched.yaml
  • rerun with --keep-containers --verbose
./bin/meridian test -c collector.yaml --keep-containers --verbose

docs.title: Missing env values
docs.text:
Provide values through:

  • --env-file
  • repeatable --env KEY=value
  • exported shell environment variables

docs.title: Semantic validation gaps
docs.text:
If print-config is unavailable:

  • confirm the Collector build supports print-config
  • confirm it accepts otelcol.printInitialConfig
  • expect effective-config artifacts to be absent

docs.title: Engine issues
docs.text:
Validate the runtime first:

  • docker version
  • nerdctl version
  • lima nerdctl version

If auto picks the wrong engine for your environment, select --engine docker or --engine containerd explicitly.

docs.title: Compare two files directly
docs.text: ./bin/meridian diff --old old.yaml --new new.yaml

docs.title: Compare git refs
docs.text: ./bin/meridian diff --base origin/main --head HEAD --new otel/collector.yaml

docs.title: Gate runtime review to changed inputs
docs.text:
./bin/meridian check -c otel/collector.yaml --old old.yaml --new new.yaml --changed-only

--changed-only requires explicit diff inputs.

docs.title: Filter by severity
docs.text:
./bin/meridian diff --old old.yaml --new new.yaml --severity-threshold medium

Use this when you want reviewer attention focused on medium and high-risk changes only.

docs.title: 1. Build Meridian
docs.text: go build -o ./bin/meridian ./cmd/meridian

docs.title: 2. Validate a known-good example
docs.text:
./bin/meridian validate -c examples/basic/collector.yaml

If you need env interpolation, add:

./bin/meridian validate -c collector.yaml --env-file .env --env OTLP_ENDPOINT=localhost:4317

docs.title: 3. Run the end-to-end check
docs.text: ./bin/meridian check -c examples/basic/collector.yaml

docs.title: 4. Inspect the result
docs.text:
./bin/meridian debug summary
./bin/meridian debug bundle

docs.title: 5. Save machine-readable output when scripting
docs.text: ./bin/meridian ci -c examples/basic/collector.yaml --format json --json-file /tmp/report.json

docs.title: Use The GitHub Action
docs.text: Meridian ships a composite action at action/action.yml.

docs.title: Minimal usage
docs.text:
- uses: salman-frs/meridian/action@v1
  with:
    config: examples/basic/collector.yaml
    engine: auto

For local development inside this repository, use uses: ./action.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text:
• config
  • engine
  • mode
  • collector_image
  • timeout
  • env_file
  • assertions
  • render_graph
  • comment_mode
  • artifact_retention_days

docs.title: Behavior
docs.text:
The action:

  • builds the Meridian binary from the checked-out repository
  • runs meridian ci
  • uploads meridian-artifacts, summary.md, and report.json
  • writes the GitHub step summary
  • updates one PR comment marked with <!-- meridian-comment --> when comment mode is enabled

docs.title: Caveats
docs.text:
• engine: containerd is intended for Linux runners with nerdctl
  • the shipped action depends on actions/upload-artifact@v4, so GHES is not part of the current compatibility contract

docs.title: Inspect Artifacts
docs.text: Meridian's debug commands operate on the latest run by default.

docs.title: Summary
docs.text: ./bin/meridian debug summary

docs.title: Bundle manifest
docs.text:
Human form:

./bin/meridian debug bundle

JSON form:

./bin/meridian debug bundle --format json

docs.title: Collector logs
docs.text: ./bin/meridian debug logs

docs.title: Capture samples
docs.text: ./bin/meridian debug capture

docs.title: Target a specific run
docs.text: ./bin/meridian debug summary --output ./meridian-artifacts --run ./meridian-artifacts/runs/20260316-123456.000000000-4242

docs.title: K3s Regression Workflow
docs.text: Use the repo-owned fixture when validating Meridian itself against the canonical VM environment.

docs.title: Run one scenario
docs.text: scripts/e2e_k3s_vm.sh happy

docs.title: Run the full matrix
docs.text: scripts/e2e_k3s_vm.sh all

docs.title: Review scenario output
docs.text:
Artifacts are written under:

/tmp/meridian-e2e-custom/<run-id>/

Key files:

  • summary.md
  • summary.json
  • logs/*.log
  • prom-http-requests.json
  • prom-checkout.json
  • prom-heartbeat.json
  • loki-storefront.json
  • tempo-storefront.json

Remember that negative scenarios are supposed to pass when the expected failure mode is detected correctly.

docs.title: Local End-To-End Walkthrough
docs.text: This walkthrough uses the repo's basic example to exercise Meridian's normal local flow.

docs.title: 1. Build the CLI
docs.text: go build -o ./bin/meridian ./cmd/meridian

docs.title: 2. Run validation
docs.text:
./bin/meridian validate -c examples/basic/collector.yaml

Observe:

  • local-load stage result
  • semantic stage result
  • any findings grouped by severity

docs.title: 3. Run the confidence workflow
docs.text: ./bin/meridian check -c examples/basic/collector.yaml

docs.title: 4. Read the evidence
docs.text:
Open:

  • summary.md
  • report.json
  • config.patched.yaml
  • graph.mmd
  • captures/

If the selected Collector supports print-config, also inspect config.final.yaml.

docs.title: Pull Request Workflow
docs.text: This is the intended reviewer workflow for a repo that stores Collector configs in Git.

docs.title: 1. Validate locally before opening the PR
docs.text:
./bin/meridian validate -c otel/collector.yaml
./bin/meridian check -c otel/collector.yaml

docs.title: 2. Review the structural delta
docs.text: ./bin/meridian diff --base origin/main --head HEAD --new otel/collector.yaml

docs.title: 3. Enable the action in CI
docs.text:
jobs:
  meridian:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      pull-requests: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: salman-frs/meridian/action@v1
        with:
          config: otel/collector.yaml

docs.title: 4. Review the CI evidence
docs.text:
Use:

  • the GitHub step summary
  • the PR comment
  • the uploaded artifact bundle

The point is durable review evidence, not just pass/fail status.
